spoilers.text: #ONEPIECE1189 BRIEF SPOILERS FOR ONEPIECE CHAPTER 1189 BY ME

spoilers.title: #ONEPIECE1189 The spear that Imu gave to St. Killingham to use in the chapter is called "Longinus," written in kanji that means "Spear of Holy Life" In Christianity, the Spear of Longinus (named after
spoilers.text: #ONEPIECE1189 The spear that Imu gave to St. Killingham to use in the chapter is called "Longinus," written in kanji that means "Spear of Holy Life" In Christianity, the Spear of Longinus (named after Saint Longinus), also known as the Holy Lance, the Spear of Destiny, or the Holy Spear, is the lance that pierced the side of Jesus as He hung on the cross during His crucifixion. "Longinus" is the name traditionally given to the unknown Roman soldier who pierced Jesus' side with a lance. In medieval and some modern Christian traditions, he is described as a convert to Christianity. Additional Spoiler Info: The Longinus spear is the weapon of the Rimoshifu monarch from 800 years ago. It is essentially a family heirloom passed down through the Rimoshifu family.

misc.text: #ONEPIECE #ONEPIECE1189 It's this one, The only Katana out of the 19 blades More than likley a black blade WorstGenHQ (@WorstGenHQ) #ONEPIECE1189 Another weapon of the 19 Families is also mentioned; it is called "Muramasa." Muramasa (村正; born before 1501), commonly known as Sengo Muramasa, was a famous swordsmith who founded the Muramasa school and lived during the Muromachi period in Japan!!👀👀 — https://nitter.net/WorstGenHQ/status/2079893172964954418#m

misc.title: #ONEPIECE1189 The second sword Imu summons this chapter: Fragarach - (Sword of Sea God)!! "In Irish mythology, Fragarach, known as "The Answerer", was the sword of the sea god Manannán mac Lir. The sw
misc.text: #ONEPIECE1189 The second sword Imu summons this chapter: Fragarach - (Sword of Sea God)!! "In Irish mythology, Fragarach, known as "The Answerer", was the sword of the sea god Manannán mac Lir. The sword weakened its foes, and no mail or armour could block it." 👀

misc.text: US Americans in the comments are like Boruto haters: They both have a basically non existent reading comprehension #BORUTO Pop Base (@PopBase) France recorded its hottest-ever day yesterday with an average temperature of 29.8°C Around 40 people are confirmed to have drowned while swimming in efforts to cool down. (theguardian.com/world/2026/j…) Community note: The 29.8°C is France's national average of day and night temperatures across 30 weather stations; local highs reached 44.3°C. The 40 drownings occurred over the past week during the heatwave. apnews.com/article/heat-w… bbc.com/news/articles/… — https://nitter.net/PopBase/status/2069754874074398969#m
